mechanism_narrative: The federal funds rate held near the zero lower bound (0.15%) signals maximal monetary accommodation, lowering the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows and increasing risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as long as FEDFUNDS remains below 0.5%.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.15% as of 2009-04, near zero lower bound
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level and forward 20d SPX return falls below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window while FEDFUNDS remains under 0.5%, or forward returns during near-zero FEDFUNDS periods are not significantly higher than the full-sample average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the 3-month rate of decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) decelerates relative to the prior 3-month decline (i.e., job losses are shrinking in magnitude), it signals an approaching labor-market trough that precedes an equity recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: 3-month PAYEMS decline magnitude smaller than prior 3-month decline magnitude by at least 20%
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS=132414K as of 2009-04, still declining from crisis peak
failure_condition: In more than 50% of historical instances where PAYEMS decline decelerates by >=20%, the subsequent 60-day SPX return is negative, or rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window restricted to triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous stabilization of industrial production (INDPRO) and retail sales (RSAFS) - both series ceasing to decline month-over-month - jointly signals that both supply-side output and demand-side spending have bottomed, a stronger recovery signal than either series alone, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=97.0723 and RSAFS=337677 as of 2009-04, both near cycle lows
regime_hint: trough-to-recovery transition after demand and supply shock
failure_condition: When both INDPRO and RSAFS post month-over-month increases simultaneously, forward 20d SPX returns are negative or statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05) in over 50% of instances, invalidating the joint-bottom signal.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) stabilizing near a historic low after a multi-year decline signals that the residential investment component of demand has troughed, which historically precedes broader economic and equity market recovery, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days, but only when this occurs during a post-financial-crisis recessionary regime with near-zero policy rates.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST=510K as of 2009-03, near historic lows
regime_hint: post-financial-crisis recession with zero interest rate policy
failure_condition: If HOUST stabilization at cyclical lows occurs but FEDFUNDS is not near zero and forward 60d SPX returns are not positive, or if in the current regime the rank IC between HOUST level and forward returns is < 0.03 over a rolling 2-year window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained deceleration in the year-over-year growth rate of the Consumer Price Index (CPIAUCSL) signals a disinflationary trend that supports continued monetary accommodation and reduces real-rate headwinds to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=212.671 as of 2009-04, low and disinflationary trend post-crisis
failure_condition: Rank IC between CPIAUCSL YoY deceleration and forward 5d SPX returns falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or the sign of the relationship flips to negative and remains so for 12 consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Once the unemployment rate (UNRATE) crosses above 8.5%, historical evidence from prior deep recessions shows equity markets tend to price in labor-market bottoming expectations ahead of actual improvement, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as markets front-run the eventual labor recovery.
failure_condition: If forward 20d SPX returns are negative in more than 50% of months where UNRATE exceeds 8.5% across the historical sample, or rank IC < 0.03 in triggered subsample over a 3-year rolling window, hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A positive month-over-month change in retail sales (RSAFS) reflects immediate improvement in consumer spending momentum, which is quickly priced into equities, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month RSAFS change and forward 5d SPX returns is below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or effect reverses sign persistently for over 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: Joint deceleration in the rate of decline of industrial production (INDPRO) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) - both supply-side output and employment contracting at a slower pace simultaneously - indicates the real economy is approaching a cyclical trough, a signal more reliable than either series individually, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO=97.0723 and PAYEMS=132414K as of 2009-04, both still declining but pace of decline moderating
failure_condition: If joint deceleration of INDPRO and PAYEMS decline rates fails to precede a positive 60d SPX return in over 50% of historical instances, or the interaction term's marginal rank IC contribution over either single-series model is below 0.02, the hypothesis is rejected.
